function_explanation: Calculates the mean of within-chain variances for multiple sequences of values (chains).
function_docstring:
"""
Calculates the mean variance across multiple chains of data.

Args:
    chains (numpy.ndarray): A 3D numpy array where each element along the first dimension represents a sequence of data (chain).
        The shape is expected to be (C, N, M), where C is the number of chains, N is the number of observations in each chain, 
        and M is the number of variables in each observation.

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: An array representing the mean of the variances calculated along the chains for each variable.
    The shape will match the last two dimensions of the input, i.e., (N, M).
"""
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Example usage of the _within function
chains = np.random.rand(5, 100, 10)  # 5 chains, each with 100 observations of 10 variables
result = _within(chains)
print("Mean within-chain variance:", result)
